fields.description:
## Context / Problem

`inbound_probe.py` is wholly Jira-specific code living in the backend-neutral core: it builds
the Jira REST URL shape (`/rest/api/2/issue/{key}?fields=status,resolution`), sends a
basic-auth GET, resolves `JIRA_URL`/`JIRA_USER`/`JIRA_API_TOKEN` via
`adapters.jira.acli_subprocess.resolve_jira_settings()` (`inbound_probe.py:65-77`), and
classifies by Jira status names (`RESOLVED_STATUS_NAMES = {"Resolved","Done","Cancelled"}`).
It is called from `run_differs.py:369`, and its result vocabulary is consumed *neutrally* by
`reconcile_helpers.route_inbound_probe` (`reconcile_helpers.py:291-355`). A second backend
would need an entirely different probe, but there is no seam. Epic bbf1 AC3 requires zero
`adapters.jira` imports in core.

## Approach

Split vocabulary from vendor mechanics:

- The neutral vocabulary — `ProbeBranch` (4 branches), `ProbeResult`, `ProbeConfigError` —
  stays in root `inbound_probe.py`. The file must remain at the package root: it is
  location-pinned (ADR 0035 context list) and loaded by filename at
  `reconcile_helpers.py:317` (`_load("inbound_probe", "inbound_probe.py")`).
- The Jira mechanics (URL construction, basic-auth GET, GET-only invariant, status-name
  classification, JIRA_* env resolution) move to a new `adapters/jira/probe.py`.
- A new `@runtime_checkable` capability Protocol `SupportsAbsenceProbe` in `_backend.py` with
  `probe_remote(remote_id: str) -> Any` (return documented as `inbound_probe.ProbeResult`;
  `_backend.py` stays a pure stdlib+typing interface module, consistent with its existing
  loosely-typed `dict[str, Any]` style, so it does not import the vocabulary module).
  `JiraBackend` implements it by delegating to `adapters/jira/probe.py`.
- `run_differs.py:369` routes through the backend: `isinstance(backend, SupportsAbsenceProbe)`
  guarded, matching the existing capability pattern (`SupportsLinks`/`SupportsComments`/
  `SupportsIncremental` in `_backend.py`). Wiring: today the backend is loaded at
  `run_differs.py:234` — AFTER the probe dispatch call at `:230` — so the
  `_load_reconcile_backend()` call (`run_differs.py:62-75`, a pure registry/config load with
  no remote side effects) is hoisted above the probe dispatch, and the backend object is
  passed into `_run_differs_inbound_probe_dispatch` as a new `backend` parameter, replacing
  that function's direct `probe_mod.probe(...)` call at `:369`. The later load site reuses
  the already-loaded instance. A backend without the capability yields
  `ProbeResult(UNREACHABLE)` — the conservative "do not classify, leave for retry" branch
  that `route_inbound_probe` already handles (`reconcile_helpers.py:351`).
- Extend the AST neutrality gate to assert zero `adapters.jira` imports in
  `inbound_probe.py`.

Rejected alternative: injecting url/user/token through scalar port accessors and keeping the
GET + classification in core — that leaves the vendor REST URL shape and Jira status-name
classification in neutral code, so a second backend still could not drop in; the port must
own the whole probe as a capability.

## Scope

- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_backend.py (SupportsAbsenceProbe capability)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_probe.py (reduced to neutral vocabulary)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/probe.py (new: Jira GET + classification)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/backend.py (implement probe_remote)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/run_differs.py (capability-guarded routing at the probe call site)
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/fetch/test_inbound_probe.py, test_inbound_probe_dispatch.py (classification tests migrate to the adapter home)
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/fetch/test_probe_capability_routing.py (new: the capability-routing tests — backend-with-capability runtime proof, capability-lacking UNREACHABLE degradation)
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py (AST gate extended to inbound_probe.py)

## Testing

- Happy path: 200 + resolved status → PRESENT_RESOLVED; 200 + other status →
  PRESENT_FILTERED (now exercised against `adapters/jira/probe.py`).
- Failure/edge: 404/410/403 → ARCHIVED_OR_MOVED; 5xx/401/timeout/URLError → UNREACHABLE;
  missing JIRA_* env → ProbeConfigError naming the missing vars; GET-only invariant asserted
  in the adapter.
- Capability routing (new file
  `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/fetch/test_probe_capability_routing.py`): with a FakeBackend
  implementing SupportsAbsenceProbe, run_differs uses the backend's probe (runtime proof);
  with a FakeBackend lacking it, the mutation is classified UNREACHABLE and left for retry.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `inbound_probe.py` contains NO `adapters.jira` import and no vendor REST code (URL
      shape, auth, status-name classification all live under `adapters/jira/probe.py`) —
      verified by the extended AST case in
      `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py` plus code review.
- [ ] `run_differs` reaches the probe only through `SupportsAbsenceProbe`; a
      capability-lacking backend degrades to UNREACHABLE — verified by the routing tests in
      Testing.
- [ ] Probe classification behavior is byte-identical for Jira (all branches) — verified by
      the migrated probe tests.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` green; whole test tree collects with 0 errors.
